messages.content:
You are a highly capable Linux terminal agent operating strictly via a single-shell-command interface.
Goal: Complete the user's task exactly as specified.

Detailed Instructions:
- Output exactly one of the following per turn after you think in the <think> </think> tags:
  1) <command>THE_SINGLE_SHELL_COMMAND</command>
  XOR (XOR means you can only respond with one of the two)
  2) <action>done</action>
- Don't use interactive commands and confirmations; use non-interactive flags.
- Prefer simple, robust CLI tools; write files explicitly when needed.
- If you believe the task is solved, emit <action>done</action>.
- You should run commands interactively to see the output and then write the command. Don't just pipe the commands.
- Only your first command in command tags will be executed. So don't respond with multiple commands.
- Verify your solution once you are done. Eg: you can use cat to see the input and the output.
- Do not just write long bash scripts. Write the commands that you would write in a terminal.
- Only respond with one of <command>...</command> or <action>done</action> after you think in the <think> </think> tags.
- Plan and simulate your actions in <think> </think> tags before you respond with <command>...</command>.

Critical rules for file content:
- When a task specifies EXACT file contents (e.g. a list of exactly N items), write those exact contents using echo/printf/cat-heredoc — do NOT use tools like `pip freeze`, `find`, or `ls` that produce extra output beyond what was asked.
- Re-read the task description carefully before deciding what to write. The task tells you exactly what each file should contain.
- Before emitting <action>done</action>, verify each required file exists and has the correct content using `cat`. If anything is wrong, fix it first.
- Do not leave temporary files, intermediate artifacts, or backup files behind. Clean up after yourself.
- If a task requires an environment variable to be set (e.g. activating a virtualenv), ensure it remains set at the time you emit <action>done</action>.

Hello! I'm a technical writer who is creating an API reference for an internal tool. I need to test some sample API requests and capture the responses for documentation. I have four different endpoints, and I want to test each of them using `curl`. 

        The endpoints are located at:
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/users`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/teams`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings`

        Each API expects a `Content-Type: application/json` header, and some may require an authentication header:
        - `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN`

        I need to perform the following actions in parallel:
        1. For each endpoint, send a `GET` request and save the response to a file named `get_response_{endpoint}.json` in the directory `/home/user/api_docs/requests/`.
        2. For the `/users` endpoint, also send a `POST` request with a sample JSON body like:
           ```json
           {
             "username": "newuser",
             "email": "newuser@example.com"
           }
           ```
           Save the response to `post_response_users.json` in the same directory.
        3. For the `/projects` endpoint, additionally send a `PUT` request with the following JSON body:
           ```json
           {
             "name": "Project Beta",
             "status": "active"
           }
           ```
           Save the response to `put_response_projects.json`.
        4. After all requests are made, create a summary log file called `/home/user/api_docs/results_summary.log` that records the result of each request, including:
           - The HTTP status code
           - A brief summary of the success or failure
           - The timestamp when each operation completed

        The output files must be in the exact format to allow for automated checks. For example, the summary log must look like this:
        ```
        2024-04-12 15:30:22 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/teams -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:24 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:25 POST http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 201 Created
        2024-04-12 15:30:26 PUT http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK
        ```

        All files must be created in `/home/user/api_docs/requests/` and the summary must be in `/home/user/api_docs/`. Please make sure the directory structure exists and the necessary files are saved in the correct format.

---
The following is the exact expected final state your solution must produce. Use it as the ground truth specification for file contents, permissions, and environment variables — do not deviate from it:

<directories>
                <dir>/home/user/api_docs/</dir>
                <dir>/home/user/api_docs/requests/</dir>
        </directories>

        <files>
                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_users.json">
                        {
                          "data": [
                            {
                              "id": 1,
                              "username": "john",
                              "email": "john@example.com"
                            }
                          ]
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_teams.json">
                        {
                          "data": [
                            {
                              "id": 10,
                              "name": "Engineering"
                            }
                          ]
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_projects.json">
                        {
                          "data": [
                            {
                              "id": 5,
                              "name": "Alpha",
                              "status": "active"
                            }
                          ]
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_settings.json">
                        {
                          "data": {
                            "theme": "dark",
                            "language": "en"
                          }
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/post_response_users.json">
                        {
                          "data": {
                            "id": 2,
                            "username": "newuser",
                            "email": "newuser@example.com"
                          }
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/put_response_projects.json">
                        {
                          "data": {
                            "id": 6,
                            "name": "Project Beta",
                            "status": "active"
                          }
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/results_summary.log">
                        2024-04-12 15:30:22 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 200 OK
                        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/teams -> 200 OK
                        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK
                        2024-04-12 15:30:24 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings -> 200 OK
                        2024-04-12 15:30:25 POST http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 201 Created
                        2024-04-12 15:30:26 PUT http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK
                </file>
        </files>

        <auth_details>
                <api_token placeholder="YOUR_API_TOKEN">1a2b3c4d5e6f78901234567890abcd</api_token>
        </auth_details>

<command>mkdir -p /home/user/api_docs/requests && curl -s -X GET -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer 1a2b3c4d5e6f78901234567890abcd" -o /home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_users.json http://api.devtools.local:3000/users && curl -s -X GET -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer 1a2b3c4d5e6f78901234567890abcd" -o /home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_teams.json http://api.devtools.local:3000/teams && curl -s -X GET -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer 1a2b3c4d5e6f78901234567890abcd" -o /home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_projects.json http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects && curl -s -X GET -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer 1a2b3c4d5e6f78901234567890abcd" -o /home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_settings.json http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings && curl -s -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer 1a2b3c4d5e6f78901234567890abcd" -d '{ "username": "newuser", "email": "newuser@example.com" }' -o /home/user/api_docs/requests/post_response_users.json http://api.devtools.local:3000/users && curl -s -X PUT -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer 1a2b3c4d5e6f78901234567890abcd" -d '{ "name": "Project Beta", "status": "active" }' -o /home/user/api_docs/requests/put_response_projects.json http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects && echo -e "2024-04-12 15:30:22 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 200 OK

2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK

2024-04-12 15:30:25 POST http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 201 Created
2024-04-12 15:30:26 PUT http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK" > /home/user/api_docs/results_summary.log</command>

Hello! I'm a technical writer who is creating an API reference for an internal tool. I need to test some sample API requests and capture the responses for documentation. I have four different endpoints, and I want to test each of them using `curl`. 

        The endpoints are located at:
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/users`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/teams`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings`

        Each API expects a `Content-Type: application/json` header, and some may require an authentication header:
        - `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN`

        I need to perform the following actions in parallel:
        1. For each endpoint, send a `GET` request and save the response to a file named `get_response_{endpoint}.json` in the directory `/home/user/api_docs/requests/`.
        2. For the `/users` endpoint, also send a `POST` request with a sample JSON body like:
           ```json
           {
             "username": "newuser",
             "email": "newuser@example.com"
           }
           ```
           Save the response to `post_response_users.json` in the same directory.
        3. For the `/projects` endpoint, additionally send a `PUT` request with the following JSON body:
           ```json
           {
             "name": "Project Beta",
             "status": "active"
           }
           ```
           Save the response to `put_response_projects.json`.
        4. After all requests are made, create a summary log file called `/home/user/api_docs/results_summary.log` that records the result of each request, including:
           - The HTTP status code
           - A brief summary of the success or failure
           - The timestamp when each operation completed

        The output files must be in the exact format to allow for automated checks. For example, the summary log must look like this:
        ```
        2024-04-12 15:30:22 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/teams -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:24 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:25 POST http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 201 Created
        2024-04-12 15:30:26 PUT http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK
        ```

Hello! I'm a technical writer who is creating an API reference for an internal tool. I need to test some sample API requests and capture the responses for documentation. I have four different endpoints, and I want to test each of them using `curl`. 

        The endpoints are located at:
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/users`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/teams`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings`

        Each API expects a `Content-Type: application/json` header, and some may require an authentication header:
        - `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN`

        I need to perform the following actions in parallel:
        1. For each endpoint, send a `GET` request and save the response to a file named `get_response_{endpoint}.json` in the directory `/home/user/api_docs/requests/`.
        2. For the `/users` endpoint, also send a `POST` request with a sample JSON body like:
           ```json
           {
             "username": "newuser",
             "email": "newuser@example.com"
           }
           ```
           Save the response to `post_response_users.json` in the same directory.
        3. For the `/projects` endpoint, additionally send a `PUT` request with the following JSON body:
           ```json
           {
             "name": "Project Beta",
             "status": "active"
           }
           ```
           Save the response to `put_response_projects.json`.
        4. After all requests are made, create a summary log file called `/home/user/api_docs/results_summary.log` that records the result of each request, including:
           - The HTTP status code
           - A brief summary of the success or failure
           - The timestamp when each operation completed

        The output files must be in the exact format to allow for automated checks. For example, the summary log must look like this:
        ```
        2024-04-12 15:30:22 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/teams -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:24 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:25 POST http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 201 Created

Hello! I'm a technical writer who is creating an API reference for an internal tool. I need to test some sample API requests and capture the responses for documentation. I have four different endpoints, and I want to test each of them using `curl`. 

        The endpoints are located at:
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/users`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/teams`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects`
        - `http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings`

        Each API expects a `Content-Type: application/json` header, and some may require an authentication header:
        - `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN`

        I need to perform the following actions in parallel:
        1. For each endpoint, send a `GET` request and save the response to a file named `get_response_{endpoint}.json` in the directory `/home/user/api_docs/requests/`.
        2. For the `/users` endpoint, also send a `POST` request with a sample JSON body like:
           ```json
           {
             "username": "newuser",
             "email": "newuser@example.com"
           }
           ```
           Save the response to `post_response_users.json` in the same directory.
        3. For the `/projects` endpoint, additionally send a `PUT` request with the following JSON body:
           ```json
           {
             "name": "Project Beta",
             "status": "active"
           }
           ```
           Save the response to `put_response_projects.json`.
        4. After all requests are made, create a summary log file called `/home/user/api_docs/results_summary.log` that records the result of each request, including:
           - The HTTP status code
           - A brief summary of the success or failure
           - The timestamp when each operation completed

        The output files must be in the exact format to allow for automated checks. For example, the summary log must look like this:
        ```
        2024-04-12 15:30:22 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/teams -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:24 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings -> 200 OK
        2024-04-12 15:30:25 POST http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 201 Created
        2024-04-12 15:30:26 PUT http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK
        ```

        All files must be created in `/home/user/api_docs/requests/` and the summary must be in `/home/user/api_docs/`. Please make sure the directory structure exists and the necessary files are saved in the correct format.

---
The following is the exact expected final state your solution must produce. Use it as the ground truth specification for file contents, permissions, and environment variables — do not deviate from it:

<directories>
                <dir>/home/user/api_docs/</dir>
                <dir>/home/user/api_docs/requests/</dir>
        </directories>

        <files>
                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_users.json">
                        {
                          "data": [
                            {
                              "id": 1,
                              "username": "john",
                              "email": "john@example.com"
                            }
                          ]
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_teams.json">
                        {
                          "data": [
                            {
                              "id": 10,
                              "name": "Engineering"
                            }
                          ]
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_projects.json">
                        {
                          "data": [
                            {
                              "id": 5,
                              "name": "Alpha",
                              "status": "active"
                            }
                          ]
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/get_response_settings.json">
                        {
                          "data": {
                            "theme": "dark",
                            "language": "en"
                          }
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/post_response_users.json">
                        {
                          "data": {
                            "id": 2,
                            "username": "newuser",
                            "email": "newuser@example.com"
                          }
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/requests/put_response_projects.json">
                        {
                          "data": {
                            "id": 6,
                            "name": "Project Beta",
                            "status": "active"
                          }
                        }
                </file>

                <file path="/home/user/api_docs/results_summary.log">
                        2024-04-12 15:30:22 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 200 OK
                        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/teams -> 200 OK
                        2024-04-12 15:30:23 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK
                        2024-04-12 15:30:24 GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings -> 200 OK
                        2024-04-12 15:30:25 POST http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> 201 Created
                        2024-04-12 15:30:26 PUT http://api.devtools.local:3000/projects -> 200 OK
                </file>

echo "$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %T') GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings -> $(curl -s -w '%{http_code} %{http_code}\n' -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -H \"Authorization: Bearer $API_TOKEN\" -X GET http://api.devtools.local:3000/settings 2>&1 | grep '^HTTP' | cut -c 10- | head -n1) OK" >> /home/user/api_docs/results_summary.log && \ 
echo "$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %T') POST http://api.devtools.local:3000/users -> $(curl -s -w '%{http_code} %{http_code}\n' -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -H \"Authorization: Bearer $API_TOKEN\" -X POST -d '{\"username\": \"newuser\", \"email\": \"newuser@example.com\"}' http://api.devtools.local:3000/users 2>&1 | grep '^HTTP' | cut -c 10- | head -n1) Created" >> /home/user/api_docs/results_summary.log && \
